rules.rule_text:
title: Uncommon Microsoft Oauth2 API call
id: adc9d94f-7c57-4ed1-8711-5afc12fd6fed
description: Uncommon Microsoft Oauth2 API call observed to renew access token by
  BlueBravo
status: experimental
author: NTT Security - Axel Olsson
date: '2023-08-02'
references:
- https://go.recordedfuture.com/hubfs/reports/cta-2023-0727-1.pdf
- https://blog.cluster25.duskrise.com/2022/09/23/in-the-footsteps-of-the-fancy-bear-powerpoint-graphite/
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri: /common/oauth2/v2.0/token
    cs-referer: ''
  filter:
    c-useragent|contains:
    - Mozilla/5.0 (Windows
  condition: selection and not filter
falsepositives:
- Legitimate requests to renew the token using this rare API path, investigate the
  traffic from the host if it looks to be from a legitimate product or if it looks
  suspicious.
- It could be a sign of exfiltration and/or C2 communication if it is not seen in
  relation to web surf or other legitimate activity.
level: medium
tags:
- attack.command_and_control
